title: festuca caerulea var. litoralis
intro: Festuca caerulea var. litoralis, also known as Blue Fescue, is a type of perennial grass native to coastal areas. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other compounds. The health benefits of Festuca caerulea var. litoralis include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a useful natural remedy for various health issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety in human use.
